entry.title: Re: Error 403 when Create Sales Order using API_SALES_ORD...
entry.content: Hi @WG23 That would simplify your work as Cloud Integration can help you integrate both systems and you can avoid unnecessary enhancements.

entry.title: Re: Error 403 when Create Sales Order using API_SALES_ORD...
entry.content: Hi @JG_LEE, I am no pro in SAP Build Apps; I know a few years back I solved it with a workaround (at the time of transition from AppGyver to SAP), but these days, I believe the destination and connectivity service support GET/POST/HEAD operations. Please open a new query and tag it under SAP Build Apps so my colleagues can support it. But you understand the problem, right? The issue is that you need to re-use the same session (cookies). It is not enough to pass the correct x-csrf value.

entry.title: Re: The “onAfterDataEntryProcess” event in SAP Analytics Cloud Optimized Story
entry.content: Is this similar to the BPC Standard's "Write-Back BAdI" functionality?

entry.title: need guidance to connect SAP Business One Service Layer with SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
I am trying to make a SAPUI5 application on top of the SAP Business One Service Layer,
 and I am following this tutorial 
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-extensions-for-sap-business-one-and-the-web-client/setting-up-an-sap-btp-destination-for-service-laye

i have created a Destination in SAP Cloud Platform (Cloud Foundry Environment), but I am getting the Failure reason: "Could not check at the moment. Please try again later"

The same configuration works fine in that tutorial.

Any clues?

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Deprecation of VBScript, its Impact on SAP GUI Scripting and Resulting Strategy
entry.content:
Hello Mattia @bergom,

I think your approach is very good and you can take it easy, but the horizon is getting closer. With the upcoming release of Windows 11 24H2 is VBScript available as a feature on demand (FOD) enabled by default. It is planned in two to three years as FOD but disabled by default and much later to remove it.

Just as VBScript is available to you today, you can also use JScript. Call

wscript yourJavaScriptFile.js

in your console.

JScript offers a COM interface like VBScript, so it is very easy to use SAP GUI Scripting with JScript. Here an example code.

function main() {

  try {

    var SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI");
    if (
      !SapGuiAuto || SapGuiAuto === "null" || SapGuiAuto === "undefined"
    ) {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get object SAPGUI");
      return;
    }

    var app = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine;
    if (!app || app === "null" || app === "undefined") {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get scripting engine");
      return;
    }

    app.HistoryEnabled = false;

    var connection = app.Children(0);
    if (
      !connection || connection === "null" || connection === "undefined"
    ) {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get connection");
      return;
    }

    if (connection.DisabledByServer === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Connection disabled by server");
      return;
    }

    var session = connection.Children(0);
    if (!session || session === "null" || session === "undefined") {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get session");
      return;
    }

    if (session.Busy === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Session busy");
      return;
    }

    if (session.Info.IsLowSpeedConnection === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Session has low speed connection");
      return;
    }

    session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nse16";
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0);
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDATABROWSE-TABLENAME").text = "TADIR";
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDATABROWSE-TABLENAME").caretPosition = 5;
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0);
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(31);
    session.findById("wnd[1]/tbar[0]/btn[0]").press();
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(3);
    session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[3]").press();

    app.HistoryEnabled = true;

  } catch (exception) {
    if (!exception.description) {
      WScript.Echo("An exception " + (exception.number & 0xFFFF) +
        " occurred");
    } else {
      WScript.Echo("An exception " + (exception.number & 0xFFFF) +
        " occurred:\n" + exception.description);
    }
  }

}

main();

Sure, the syntax differs between VBScript and JScript but the basic approach remains the same. In my opinion, learning a new programming language is not that difficult if you have already mastered a similar one. Approach things calmly, do not let it drive you crazy and you will be successful after a short time. You can do this now, regardless whether the backend uses SAP HANA or not.

I have no doubt that you will make it.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Overall timeout/deadline for CPI iflow
entry.content:
Hi @juankinspain 

Unfortunately, there is no functionality out of the box to achieve what you seek. As you described, any solution will need to be a combination of scripts so you can achieve it with code and loops until you find the correct condition.

I want to share some guidelines because I have been in a similar situation where iFlows have been running for some time (more than an hour), and in those cases, I reached out to product support (support.sap.com) because it was not working as I expected.

  1. It is not recommended to have iFlows, which require hours to complete, especially if the iFlows are not restartable (that means if it fails, it can be restarted from the point of failure). Ideally, the logic should be split into multiple iFlows, which, all together, produce the required outcome. Ideally, these sub-flows should be designed to be restartable, so even if some flow fails, it gets eventually restarted and completed. The more time an iFlow takes, the bigger the probability that it fails. Reasons:
  1. iFlow runs out of memory, and the iFlow gets killed
  2. Any other iFlow on the node produces an out-of-memory situation, and all iFlows running at the same node get killed
  3. During software updates or security patches, an iFlow takes 5 minutes to complete; afterward, it gets killed

I hope this helps you.
Best regards 🖖🏻
Ricardo

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi,

I am from Germany, but originally from Latvia. My main expertise is ABAP development. Looking forward to learn from community!

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance
entry.content:
Dear YO-YO ,

There are two separate offerings in the SAP Cloud Appliance Library catalogue from Q4 2024:

  • One appliance template with pre-configured and activated SAP S/4HANA Fiori UI, named “SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02” ( link )
  • And another appliance template with pre-activated SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA and demo data and scenarios on top, named “SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance” ( link )

You can take a look at their description and getting started guides (both can be found following the links provided above) to gain a better understanding of what each template brings and decide which will suit your needs best.

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

Issue Assign Roles From Trust Configuration in Account Trial
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I wan know what is the reason that in my Accout Trial the "Default Identity Provide" Appear disabled, 

The first image is as appear in the blog and tutorials and the second as appear in my account trial.

 

I should some service?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Make custom field clickable and redirect to another app
entry.content:
Hello JustinC1,

It is possible to add a custom field at the Journal Entry Item level with the field type defined as a "Web Address." This custom field can then be populated using a BAdI during the creation or update of a journal entry. However, the field be configured to be clickable, but it is not possible to assign a specific label to this field, it will look like complete URL.

KR,

Aliaksandra

entry.title: BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration and Application Deployment Steps to CF and on-premise
entry.content:
SAP BTP Trial Account, BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration, and Application Deployment Steps

The Importance of SAP BTP and Integration: A Robust Foundation for Digital Transformation

In the era of digital transformation, having a flexible and reliable IT infrastructure is essential for businesses to enhance efficiency and maintain a competitive edge. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a modern cloud platform designed to meet these demands, enabling businesses to accelerate innovation, optimize processes, and develop new digital solutions.

A key component of this platform is SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), which provides developers with a powerful and user-friendly development environment. With BAS, creating, developing, and deploying cloud-based applications becomes faster and more efficient.

By integrating tools like SAP Cloud Connector, businesses can seamlessly connect on-premise systems with SAP BTP, ensuring secure and compliant data exchange. This integration supports hybrid system architectures, allowing companies to leverage their existing ABAP systems while benefiting from the flexibility and scalability of the cloud.

This comprehensive guide outlines the step-by-step process for setting up SAP BTP, configuring BAS, integrating with on-premise systems, and deploying applications to both Cloud Foundry and ABAP environments. Whether you're exploring SAP BTP for the first time or aiming to streamline your development processes, this document serves as a valuable resource for navigating the complexities of modern enterprise technology.

Creating Subaccount in a BTP Trial Account and Adding Services

  1. Visit https://account.hana.ondemand.com/ to create a BTP trial account.
  2. After registering, navigate to the Trial Home page and proceed to the Trial Account section to create a subaccount.
  3. Access the subaccount you created and load the required contents via the Entitlements section.
    • First, click the Edit button.
    • Then, click the Add Service Plans button to add the necessary services.

       



Business Application Studio Setup and Required Content Installation

To set up Business Application Studio (BAS) and install the necessary content, you can follow either of these two approaches:

  1. Adding the Service to an Existing Subaccount
    If you have already created a subaccount, you can add Business Application Studio to it by selecting the service addition option. Alternatively, you can directly go to the BOOSTER menu, search for "Business App Studio," or find it in the list. From there, you can create the subaccount while setting up BAS.
  2. Setting Up the Services
    After the installation is complete, go to the Entitlements menu, click Configure Entitlements, and add the following services in the order listed:
    • SAP Build Work Zone
    • SAP Business Application Studio
    • SAP HANA Cloud
    • SAP HANA Schemas & HDI Containers
    • Mobile Services
    • Cloud Foundry Runtime/Environment
  3. Creating the Business Application Studio Instance
    Next, create the BAS instance from the Instance menu.
  4. Running Business Application Studio
    Once BAS is installed and the services are configured, start the Business Application Studio.
  5. Creating a New Workspace
    After running BAS, create a new workspace for your project.
  6. Ready to Use
    Your Business Application Studio is now ready to use.

     

This setup process ensures that all required services are added and that your BAS environment is configured and running, ready for your development work.

 

Installing and Configuring SAP Cloud Connector

After setting up Business Application Studio (BAS), if you wish to integrate it with an ABAP system that uses a VPN, you will need to create a connection point using SAP Cloud Connector. SAP Cloud Connector provides the following functionalities:

  • Secure connection between SAP BTP and on-premise systems.
  • Controls data flow between systems.
  • Ensures compliance with security policies.

You can download the SAP Cloud Connector installation file from the following link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

Installation on Windows:

  1. Double-click the installation file to start the process.
  2. Accept the license agreement.
  3. Choose the installation directory (e.g., C:\SAP\CloudConnector).
  4. Click Install and wait for the installation to complete.

Step 1: Starting the Cloud Connector

  1. Navigate to Start > SAP Cloud Connector to launch the application.
  2. It will automatically open in your default browser. Alternatively, you can manually open the URL: http://localhost:8443 .

Step 2: Logging In

  • Username: Administrator
  • Password: manage

If you have previously logged in and changed your password but forgot it, you can reset the password by editing the users.xml file located in the C:\SAP\scc20\config directory. Change the password field to the encrypted value: "3F282FCBA8933E03A65A6DC92A27DE8396961E2F". This will reset the password to manage. After saving the file, restart SAP Cloud Connector from the Services menu.

Step 3: Configuring the SAP BTP Connection

  1. To establish a connection between the subaccount you created in SAP BTP and Cloud Connector, go to the Cloud Connector Administration panel and click Add Subaccount.

     

  2. Skip the page requesting host, port, user, and password. Enter the subaccount details from your SAP BTP account.


  3. Navigate to the Cloud To On-Premise tab.
  4. Click the Add System Mapping button and fill in the following information:
    • Backend Type: ABAP System
    • Protocol: HTTPS or HTTP
    • Host: e.g., 10.10.10.10
    • Port: e.g., 8000
  5. Click Save and test the connection.

Mapping Configuration:

  • When creating the system mapping, if you enter '/' in the URL path field, all subdirectories will be included automatically, eliminating the need to specify each subdirectory individually.

With these steps completed, SAP Cloud Connector will be configured and ready for use in integrating your ABAP system with SAP BTP.

 

Establishing a Connection Between BAS Workspace and ABAP System

After creating the connection point for your on-premise system (e.g., TST system) using Cloud Connector, you can establish a connection between your Business Application Studio (BAS) workspace and the system in two ways:

Method 1: Direct Connection from Business Application Studio

  1. In BAS, go to the Service section from the left-hand menu.
  2. Select Add New Service under your account and enter the required details.
  3. After entering the details, click on the service. If a green button appears in the corner, it means the information was entered correctly and the connection is established successfully.

Method 2: Setting Up the Destination in BTP Account

  1. Log in to your BTP account and select your subaccount.
  2. In the left-hand menu, select Connectivity, then choose Destinations.
  3. Before proceeding, click on Cloud Connectors and verify if the access point you created in the Cloud Connector application appears under Exposed Back-End Systems as "available."
  4. Now, from the Destinations section, click Create Destination to create a destination for the service.
  5. Fill in the following details:
    • https://adress:port
    • SAP Client, User, and Password: Provide the credentials for your ABAP backend system.
    • Web IDE System: This should match the information for your ABAP backend system.
    • URL: Enter the URL in the format:
    • Proxy Type: Set to OnPremise.
    • Authentication: Set to BasicAuthentication.
  6. In the Web IDE Usage field, add the following values:
    • odata_abap: For Gateway operations.
    • ui5_execute_abap: To execute UI5 applications from the ABAP repository.
    • dev_abap: For development and deployment operations.
      If additional functionalities are required, you can search for other Web IDE usages by querying WebIDEUsage.
  7. After entering the details, click Check to verify the connection.

     

Once the connection is successfully verified, your BAS workspace will be connected to the ABAP system.

 

Deploying the Application to On-Premise System or Cloud Foundry

After completing the project, open the project directory in the terminal and run the following commands:

  1. npm install
  2. npm build

This will create the node_modules and dist folders.

Then, run the following command:

  • npm run deploy-config
    • or alternatively:
    • npx fiori add deploy-config

At this point, we have two options for deployment: Cloud Foundry or ABAP system.

Option 1: Deploying to Cloud Foundry

  1. When deploying to Cloud Foundry, select CF and it will prompt you for your Cloud Foundry account credentials.
  2. Enter your BTP account email and password. Once entered, it will establish the connection to Cloud Foundry, and if successful, you will be able to deploy the application.
  3. Before proceeding, ensure that the Cloud Foundry service is added and running in your BTP account. When you enter the Cloud Foundry section in your BTP Subaccount, you should see your space with deployed applications.
  4. Additionally, if your application is published as an HTML5 app, it will also appear in the HTML5 Applications section in the left-hand menu of your BTP Subaccount.

Option 2: Deploying to ABAP System

Before starting, create a Transport Request number in your ABAP system.

  1. In BAS, select the ABAP system in the terminal section.
  2. You will then be prompted to choose the destination (the service-destination you added earlier).
  3. Next, you will be asked for the SAPUI5 ABAP Repository. Enter the name of the BSP Application you created under the package.
  4. Afterward, you will be asked for the Deployment Description. Enter your description.
  5. Then, you will be prompted for the Package. Select Enter Manually and provide the name of the package you created.
  6. Finally, input the Transport Request number and complete the process.

Even if the deploy-config is created for the ABAP system, BAS may ask for additional information, and the generated files may not be sufficient to complete the deployment. Therefore, follow these steps:

  1. Open the ui5-deploy.yaml file in your project.
  2. Under the configuration: section, add the following:

credentials:

  username: env:USERNAME

  password: env:PASSWORD

  1. Under the target: section, add:

client: env:CLIENTNUMBER

auth: basic

  1. Save the file.

After making these changes, run npm run deploy in the terminal, and your project will be deployed to the ABAP system with the necessary configurations.

Check SE80 to verify that the project has been successfully deployed.

Lastly, in the SICF menu, enter the name of your BSP application and confirm that the application service is running correctly.

entry.title: Preparing for the Next Big Step: SAP Certification Ahead!
entry.content:
Hey SAP Community!

I’m thrilled to share that I’ve completed the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Bootcamp and am now gearing up to take the certification exam! This marks a pivotal moment in my career journey as I prepare to officially become an SAP Certified Associate.

During this transformative program, I gained hands-on experience with the SAP Live System and built critical skills such as system configuration, business process integration, and data migration. These experiences have equipped me with the knowledge and confidence to take on new challenges in the SAP ecosystem.

What’s Next?
After certification, I’m looking forward to exploring opportunities in industries like finance, supply chain, manufacturing, or cloud computing and applying SAP solutions to drive innovation and efficiency.

I’m excited for what’s ahead and eager to connect with others in the SAP community to collaborate and grow together! 🚀

#SAPJourney #CertificationReady #CareerGrowth #S4HANACloud #SAPCommunity #FutureOfWork

entry.title: Re: Query List of Otbound Deliveries from SAP S/4 Hana Pu...
entry.content:
Hi @Hanumath 
I will assume you have configured the corresponding communication scenario.
The URI is the following:

 

https://myxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002/A_OutbDeliveryHeader?$top=3&$filter=CreationDate ge datetime'2016-08-16T00:00:00'&$inlinecount=allpages

 

I recommend using the encoded format

 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002/A_OutbDeliveryHeader?$top=3&$filter=CreationDate%20ge%20datetime'2024-12-16T00%3A00%3A00'&$inlinecount=allpages

 

I hope this helps you.

Best regards 🖖🏻
Ricardo

entry.title: Re: Can we restrict some Web Services ID for External Catalog in Manage Purchase Requisition - Pro a

entry.title: Request to add a column in Tax Reconciliation Account Balance App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

I would like to request the addition of the supplier number/name to Tax Reconciliation Account Balance App. This is already a highly useful report for tax advance analysis, but including the supplier details would make it even more effective and comprehensive.

Adding this information would enhance the report's functionality and provide better insights, ultimately improving our analysis and decision-making processes.

Thank you for considering this request. I look forward to your feedback.

Best regards,

entry.title: Sap public cloud - PIRS
entry.content:

Other plants are not searched.
Where do I set the default settings?
I want to search other plants as well.

Hi @GoutamBudha,

This feature is available from S/4HANA on-premise edition 2021 onwards. Maybe you are on a lower product release?

Grace

entry.title: How to Configure Limit Attachment file Size in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Dear All,

How to setting for limit attachment file size in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, example i want upload document PR/PO but i want to limit file size for the document . Because in user profile setting, there no option for limit attachment file. Can anyone help me for this issue?

Regards,

entry.title: Rental condition type should auto calculate the unit price in the manage contract app.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement in RE FX lease accounting, where the rental condition type should auto calculate the unit price in the manage contract app.

Current standard process:

When we insert the condition type in the “Manage Contract” app, rental condition will be added, and unit price is updated manually in the section as per below screen shot.

 

Selecting the relevant condition from the list:

When we add the condition, the unit price will show as 0 as per the standard functionality.

We enter the condition amount manually in the unit price section.

 

As per our requirement for the automate calculation based on the condition type and company code combination: The Unit Price needs to be calculated automatically based on a formula...

X represents Turbine rent

A represents No. of Wind Turbines (value of 6)

B represents MW capacity of Wind Turbines (value of 20,0000)

X will be calculated as follows (A x B = X)

6 x 20,000 = 120,000

So, X (Turbine rent) will have an automatic calculated value of 120,000.

Please let us know, what is the best way to achieve this requirement. 

your prompt response would be highly appreciated 😊

Thanks,

Karthik

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

SAP Real Estate Management

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: new partner id definition, alternative partner, bypass option

entry.title: Seeking a dependency for SAP API Proxy bundle generation similar to openapi2apigee.
Re: SUM SP19: Password for 'sapsso' does not work, please reenter
entry.content:
Execute the following command as user <sid>adm from the command prompt:


rsecssfx list


Check the listed output for the following entries:
DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_USER
DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_PASSWORD

 

Execute below commands with sidadm:

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_USER sapsso -plain
rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_PASSWORD <password>

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SADB_USER sapsa -plain

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SADB_PASSWORD <Password>

entry.title: Achieving Sustainability With SAP Asset Performance Management By Leveraging Industrial Systems.
entry.content:
This blog focuses on the process of setting up Industrial Systems in SAP APM (Asset Performance Management). As part of SAP APM 2411 release, SAP introduced a new application named Industrial Systems to structure and track individual system elements within larger system setups.

The Industrial System is a master data object in SAP Asset Performance Management and is based on industry standard IEC/ISO-81346 for systems that describe several aspects of a technical system. An Industrial System is a set of inter-related objects considered in a defined context as a whole and enables functional, product-related, and location-related views of a technical system.

What is IEC/ISO 81346 Standard:

IEC 81346-1 establishes general principles for the structuring of systems including structuring of information about systems. It also provides rules and guidance for the formulation of unambiguous reference designations for objects in any systems. 

IEC 81346-2 establishes classification schemes with defined object classes and their associated letter codes, and primarily intended for use in reference designations. Designators give an unambiguous way of identifying the aspect element in the system.

Fact Sheet:

IEC International Standards provide the technical foundation that allows countries to increase national innovation and economic productivity as well as upgrade and retrofit cleaner and environmentally sound technologies and industrial processes.

The IEC 81346 standards contribute to Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including  SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) & 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities).

    

In essence,  these standards foster innovation and promote sustainable industrialization by building resilient infrastructure.

Before we dive in to understand industrial systems in SAP Asset Performance Management, we must first understand LEGO bricks. So, here we go.

LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169

Building the LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169System within the System...

The crux of the Industrial Systems is, the component systems create technical systems and those technical systems create main systems.

Let us dive in...

First things first, enable the Industrial Systems Configuration toggle for Industrial Systems in the Configuration Manager Application and created a reference designator library.

Industry Domain describes the area in which a particular industrial system operates. For instance, I have created MFG - Manufacturing & PRO - Process Industry in addition to the pre-shipped industry domains. Industry domains are a mandatory part of reference designator libraries, which are structured according to IEC/ISO 81346.

Reference Designator Source is the classification standard for the reference designator. 

Aspect is the way of viewing a system in a specified way. A system can contain more than one aspect, with each aspect describing different views of the same system.

We can create any aspect, but it must be assigned to one of the pre-shipped standard aspect types.

A relationship type is defined as the type of relationship between system elements. Relationship type holds the metadata for a relationship, such as source and target entity types, applicable aspect types (Function, Product, Location).

Note: Creation of new relationship types is not supported with the current version of Industrial Systems.

After completing the "Configuration Manager" set up, let us proceed to create "Reference Designator Libraries".

Reference designators are identifiers based on an industry standard library. A Reference Designator Library in SAP Asset Performance Management contains multiple Reference Designator Object Classes.

Note:

  • Schema Category "Aspect Oriented" can only be applied to system elements  of a selected "Aspect Type".
  • Schema Category "Object Oriented" can be applied to all system elements  independent of the "Aspect Type". -  For instance, I have chosen "Object Oriented".

Once the data is updated, we can change the status to "Released" for further processing. The status "Reopened" can be used to edit after the released status.

Now, Let us create the "Industrial System".

Here is my business use case:

I have chosen the "Cooling Water Circulation system" , which is crucial in the Calcination Process Industry. To define hierarchical structure for the "Cooling Tower System" in my calcination plant as per IEC 81346, I have classified the system based on Function, Product and Location aspects.

  • Functional Aspect: Heat Dissipation, Water Circulation, Drive Function and Control & Regulation.
  • Product Aspect: Heat Dissipation System, Pump System1, Pump System2 & Pump System3.
  • Location Aspect: Power Control Center, Motor Control Center, EX-01 Panel , EX-02 Panel & EX-03 Panel, Cooling Tower, Pump House-1.

Also, for instance, I have considered the industrial system perspective "Plant" to represent cooling water circulation system, i.e. Cooling Tower.

  • Equipment: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Functional Location: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Plant: If we want to create an asset structure for the production plant.

The prefix signs used to indicate the type of "Aspect" in a reference designator code, i.e.

  • = When relating to the function aspect of an object.
  • - When relating to the product aspect of the object.
  • + When relating to the location aspect of the object.

Function Aspect:

Product Aspect:

Location Aspect:

Adding a Product Individual to a Product Aspect. Product individual represents a technical object (equipment or functional location) present in SAP ERP, which instantiates a system element of product aspect type.

Adding a relationship between the system elements and another node.

Mini Note: The connecting arrows only point in the direction of the primary relationships, i.e. Child Of, Realized By, Located at, and Instantiated By.

We can view the basic details of the system element.

This brings me to the end of my process walk-through. I hope my blog proves useful to reliability engineering team, and I look forward seeing them use SAP APM Industrial System application to foster sustainable maintenance operations.

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. Chelikam


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

My LinkedIn Profile: www.linkedin.com/in/pardhreddyc

entry.title: Costo Estándar SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Buen día Colegas, gusto en saludarles en esta época. 

En un ambiente de prueba en SAP S/4 Hana, estoy analizado una programación de costo estándar para un material producto terminado a través de la CK40N, en el análisis observo que el kg por ejemplo se programa a $1.40/kg (validado en MM03) y en la etapa de análisis de la CK40N, refleja que el costo a programar será de $1.36/Kg. ¿Alguien sabe si programando el costo final del producto terminado con la CK40N algún paso se está saltando para que resulte esa diferencia $1.40 vs $1.36? No sé si será por los costos de la receta del sku terminado.

Agradezco los comentarios al respecto. Feliz fin de año 2024 y mis mejores deseos para este año nuevo 2025.

entry.title: Re: hxehost:53075 not responding
entry.content: Hi All,

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM quarterly update (Implementation)
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM provides quarterly updates to enhance implementation capabilities. These updates review the functionalities delivered in the previous quarter, offering insights directly from subject matter experts. The next sessions are scheduled for January 29 and 30, 2025, focusing on Q4/2024 developments.
SAP EVENTS

For detailed information on past updates, including presentations and recordings, please visit the official SAP Cloud ALM quarterly updates page.
SAP EVENTS

To stay informed about upcoming sessions and access previous materials, consider registering for the webinars and reviewing the available resources.
SAP SUPPORT PORTAL

entry.title: Open new Fiscal year
entry.content: In the SAP S/4HANA public cloud version, is the opening of a new fiscal year automatic? Is the opening of a new fiscal year for Fixed Assets (FA) also automatic? I seem to be unable to find anything to check.

entry.title: Re: CAP Node.js Plugins (1 of 3) - How things work
entry.content: CAP Node.js Plugins (1 of 3) - A deep dive into the mechanics of plugins in CAP. Understand how to extend functionality, integrate custom logic, and leverage CAP's modular design for streamlined development. Stay tuned for parts 2 and 3!

entry.title: Re: Wage type retroactive break up in Pay statement
entry.content:
Hi Babar,

I hope you are doing well.

Even i have the same requirement i.e, to show the retro wage types with the retro period and its respective amount in the pay slip form. I referred your blog and configured the same in our client system but it is not showing retro wage types, amount and its respective period in the payslip form.

Note: We have already configured the arrears wage type in our client but it does not show the retro period. In this case, do we need to stops the retro arrears wage types and follow your steps or it will work in both the cases?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: hxehost:53075 not responding
entry.content: Hi All, I believe it is the issue with VM (oracle box and vmware workstation with network adaptor or something). I tried all i am able to ping the host but I am not able to reach to the ports for webide or xs ports. Strange. I tried changing to different hostname as per blog from Lucia etc but no use and no help.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM: sequencing picking WTs for multiple Stock Ty...
entry.content:
Hi,

"Is there a way, within standard configuration, to ensure that all picking tasks are sequenced according to the pick path, regardless of stock type?"

The sorting of the warehouse tasks is defined by the profile you defined in the node "Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks" and assigned to the warehouse order creation rule in the field "WO Sorting". So what have you got there? But it might get messed up if you have assigned a different sort rule in the "Packaging Profile" (never tested that).

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Material is having stock only at Batch level in MMBE View
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are facing a strange issue for a material where the stock in MMBE view is showing under Batch level, but not showing stock under plant and storage location level.

Because of this issue, when trying to post the material transfer from this storage location to another storage location, getting the error "Deficit of stock in the Plant/Storage Location".  But actually stock is there at the batch level. Please consider the attached screenshot for your reference.

 

Regards,

Sridhar Madiraju.

entry.title: Re: User wise Document Series Production Order
entry.content: You can do that by selecting specific Users in window that pops-up when you click set as default button.

entry.title: Re: Vendor consignment stock vs Customer consignment stoc...
entry.content: i think the definition and consignment terms and conditions are a little confusing for you. Consignment is a relationship/partnership between a vendor and its customer. Since the stock is placed at the customer's location, there is little to no way for the supplier to know when the inventory provided has been consumed; the customer settles the invoice using MRKO. if for instance, a change were to occur in prices and conditions, both parties can work together and update it in info records.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD - WHAT IS IT ABOUT?
entry.content:
@RahulBhatia20 wrote:

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Explained in Detail

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a robust, cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) solution designed to enhance and streamline business processes across various industries. 

I am currently working as a SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Architect in the Public Sector industry and responsible for the Architecture, Design, and implementation for a large Public sector organization and can see tremendous advantages in why the Public sector would benefit from the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud edition. In my current project, we are implementing SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2408 utilizing the Central Business Configuration (CBC) tool to activate the specified industry relevant business processes.  Some of the Core Features offered by SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud solution that we are seeing in the project are as follows:

1. It comes with a Pre-Configured System: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud has pre-defined business processes that we can activate and implement. This allows organizations to adapt to the standard processes easily and without requiring any enhancements. With SAP's Central Business in place to support this, organizations can have their business processes configured from one single tool in the cloud. Central Business Configuration for their implementation. As Cloud is becoming much advanced with the use of AI & ML capabilities, public sector organizations such as UK Councils are seeing the benefits in the product and have started their journey by implementation of the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud solution to transform their existing landscape. 

2. Endless Scalability and Flexibility to activate new processes : Organizations can now activate business processes or use new add on features much easily than before. The new solution provides in depth insights related to how the process works with the help of Signavio and allows businesses to be in control of their system like never before. 

3.  Innovation : SAP keeps pushing regular updates and also keep advancing the functionalities that it offers with the latest use of AI & ML. Fiori apps are like never before, as it integrates with AI capabilities providing better view of the reporting and also enabling business users to easily learn how AI is redefining the business processes. 

4. Faster Integration :  SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud integrates much faster and effectively with other systems whether it be an existing SAP system or any other ERP or Non ERP system. SAP provides built in and readily available API's that helps organizations transition their data exchange smoothly. 

5. Impact on Environment and how AI is helping achieve Sustainability : SAP's S/4 HANA Public cloud offeres various new AI enabled features that now allows organizations to keep track of their carbon emissions and how their business processes are affecting the environment in todays fasted paced world.  With new reporting capabilities such as SAP Analytics Cloud, business can meet their sustainability goals much effectively and helps them to make better decisions in the longer run. 

As the product is getting matured further with AI and ML capabilities, it is fair to say that SAP's S/4 HANA Public Cloud has enormous benefits to offer to the Public Sector. 

I will continue to share insights from my existing project and how we can leverage the key benefits from SAP's latest offerings and AI enabled capabilities. 

Hello @RahulBhatia20 nysdmv ,
Your insights into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, particularly for the Public Sector, are invaluable! Highlighting its pre-configured systems, seamless scalability, and rapid integration truly showcases its potential to revolutionize public sector operations. Plus, SAP's focus on AI, ML, and sustainability aligns perfectly with modern organizational goals.

Are others in similar roles also seeing significant impacts from these new features? 

Any additional specifics you'd like to share about your project? 


Best Regards,
Thomas Brown

entry.title: Re: GRC HR Trigger considers all Plugin system for Change/Termination Access Requests
entry.content: Hi All,

entry.title: Re: GRC HR Trigger considers all Plugin system for Change/Termination Access Requests
entry.content: Hi All, Received a Note 3552279 from SAP, said its a code correction. Post implementing this note in our GRC system, Terminate requests were only considering system where user exists. For requests of Change I am still searching.

entry.title: How to Change Date Formats in Custom CDS Views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Statement:
In the Custom CDS View app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there are scenarios where a report requires a column with date values to be displayed in a specific format based on business needs. However, the app does not provide a direct function to change the date format. To address this, you can leverage a calculated column and utilize predefined functions such as CONCAT and SUBSTRING. By using these functions, you can manipulate the date values into the desired format.
I will demonstrate how to achieve this using an example to address such requirements effectively.

Solution:

Scenario:
Suppose there is a column in a standard CDS view containing date values in the MM/DD/YYYY format (Figure 1.1), but you need the date to be displayed in the DD-MM-YYYY format in your report.


Fig 1.1

Solution:
Step 1: Create a calculative column in a custom CDS.




Step 2: Copy the below code and paste in the code space and click on Apply.

concat( substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7 , 2 ) , concat( '-' , concat( substring(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5 , 2 ),concat( '-' , substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1 , 4 ) ) )))



Explanation of code :

The SUBSTRING function used to extracts specific parts of the string based on the start position and length.
The CONCAT function is used to combine two or more strings into a single string.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7, 2) extracts the day (characters 7 and 2 as 7 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5, 2) extracts the month (characters 5 and 6) as 5 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1, 4) extracts the year (characters 1 through 4) as 1 is start position and 4 is the length.

The CONCAT function combines these extracted parts and inserts the '-' character as a separator between them.

Step 3: Click on Check, and once the check is complete, click on Data Browser.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: How to Change Date Formats in Custom CDS Views in SAP...
entry.content: Yes i faced similar requirement and the solution you provided is  helpful Thank You @Ujaleswar1

entry.title: Re: Material is having stock only at Batch level in MMBE View
entry.content:
navigate to SPRO_ logistics general_ batch management_specify batch level and activate status management ( bath level might be unique at client level) 

note: this configuration is usually a one time configuration as specified by the client

entry.title: Re: Material is having stock only at Batch level in MMBE View
entry.content: Hence, If you choose plant level, the batch number is unique in conjunction with the respective material and the plant. If you choose the material level, the batch number is unique together with the material. At the client level, the batch number is unique in the whole client

entry.title: Re: Sales Condition Contract Manual settlement document s...
entry.content: @Hari : Thanks for your detailed explanation, my ambiguity is clear now pertinent to manual settlements.

entry.title: Re: Error in Extracter Checker
entry.content: was it solved? Please provide solution

entry.title: Re: Changing Company Details In SAP Account
entry.content: Hi Atharva_Soman, could you please provide us with the specific information you would like to update regarding the company details? Alternatively, you can provide a screenshot indicating the information you want to update. Please note that certain pieces of information, such as the company address and/or company name, can only be updated by the SAP Account Manager or through SAP Order Management. To obtain further information, kindly refer to KBA 1271545 - How to update S-user ID contact settings (phone number, e-mail address, time zone, language, etc) - SAP for Me

entry.content:
Very nice blog! Thanks so much.

entry.title: Re: Prevent user to make changes on the PO after saving
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to the solution in the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3098925

Krithika

entry.title: Re: CO01- Automatic selection of stock type
entry.content:
Greetings,

the field in production order (Goods receipt tab) is affected by "Post to inspection stock" indicator defined per inspection type on QM tab of material master data (plant dependant).

You can setup defaults by following the SPRO path:

SPRO -> Quality Management -> Quality Inspection -> Inspection Lot Creation -> Define Default Values for Inspection Type 

Best regards, M.

Your response will be greatly appreciated.

Thanks and Regards,

Shashidhar

entry.title: Learning material for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting certification
entry.content:
Hi,

Since the learning system for certification exams have changed during this year, I can't find anymore "Learning Journey's" for these topics:

  • Implementing with a Cloud Mindset, Building the Team, and Conducting Fit-to-Standard Workshops
  • Data Migration and Business Process Testing
  • Extensibility and Integration
  • Configuration and the SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • System Landscapes and Identity Access Management
  • Introduction to Cloud Computing and SAP Cloud ERP Deployment Options

It says that these topics could be found under related course code: S4C01_30, but I can't find any learning material when typing that code in the search bar. Any ideas where to look after these materials?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: ABWKONTI - Absence Quota output table data is incorrect
entry.content: Please see the Selection rules and Base entitlement screenshots. There used to be another Abs Qta-01, which is ended on 31.12.2022 and new Abs Qta-31 is created effective 01.01.2023. Kindly suggest, if anything needs to be checked on Selection rules.

entry.title: Resource Management for Project
entry.content:
Hi Everyone

SAP Project and Resource Management allows you as a resource manager to monitor and manage the resource utilization and the staffing of resource requests, ensuring optimal resource management. It allows resources to manage their profile of skills and assignments to be matched with the right kinds of projects. It also allows the project managers to create, manage, and monitor requests for resources on their projects.

This scope item is a public cloud solution delivered on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). The integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud is enabled through this scope item.

How to implement this in cloud and what is the BTP service that i need to use for this Scope item?

entry.title: Material Long Text Data Extraction in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a project requirement to extract Material Long text from STXL table to SAP Datasphere by Replication flow. I am using CDS view as source of Datasphere replication flow and looking for suggestions to achieve this requirement in Datasphere or in CDS view.
I have searched in SCN and found function module READ_TEXT can be call in CDS view by using virtual elements but my understanding is virtual elements can only be use for FIORI not for data extraction in Datasphere.

Thanks you.
Alka

entry.title: Job Roles Toggle_not shown_Manage Opportunity Marketplace
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I do not see the Job Roles toggle in the Manage Opportunity Marketplace after enabling OMP.

We have not enabled career explorer functionality which is part of CDP.

Regards,

Vinay.

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Unmanaged scenario example-Simplified
entry.content:
Hi Anjan,

Nice Blog post illustrating the example of Unmanaged. 

i feel its good for developers who just started their journey with RAP. i believe Unmanaged Scenario deals with Custom logic, in such cases, calling an API/BAPI/RFC would be adding more value to this blog post instead of posting a simple example like above (The one which you posted is really good indeed). Because that's the expectation from lots of developers like me who want to implement a real time scenario in the project level and seeking for a good blog on it. 

Anyways this blog will give good idea about the unmanaged approach. No doubt about that. 

Thanks once Again!

Regards

Sri Ram

entry.title: Re: hxehost:53075 not responding
entry.content:
Hi All,

Finally after 4 days, day and night got to resolve this issue. 

Its bit wierd and do not know if recommended to do but I had disabled the firewall at Suse.

sudo systemctl stop firewalld

After that when i again tried it worked.

Thanks

Rishav

entry.title: When install SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SR 2,DVEBMGS02 [ABAP: ACTIVE] did not start
entry.content:
An error occurred while processing option SAP Solution Manager 7.2 Support Release 2

 

So I can not install SAP Solution Manager 7.2 Support Release 2  

 

ctsrvs4mc01:/ # su - smaadm
ctsrvs4mc01:smaadm 301> sapcontrol -nr 02 -function GetProcessList

30.12.2024 07:53:54
GetProcessList
OK
name, description, dispstatus, textstatus, starttime, elapsedtime, pid
disp+work, Dispatcher, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:36, 9:12:18, 11015
igswd_mt, IGS Watchdog, GRAY, Stopped, , , 11016
gwrd, Gateway, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:37, 9:12:17, 11018
icman, ICM, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:37, 9:12:17, 11019
ctsrvs4mc01:smaadm 302>

entry.title: Re: When install SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SR 2,DVEBMGS02 [ABAP: ACTIVE] did not start
entry.content: Define Parameters Review Parameters Execute Service Service Completed 76% copying /data/sapcd/solman7.2/COMMON/REPORTING/UndeploymentResults.xsl Error An error occurred while processing option SAP Solution Manager 7.2 Support Release 2 > SAP Solution Manager 7.2 ABAP Support Release 2 > SAP HANA Database > Installation > Application Server ABAP > Standard System > Standard System (Last error reported by the step: ABAP processes of instance SMA/DVEBMGS02 [ABAP: ACTIVE] did not start after 10:10 minutes. Giving up). You can now: Choose Retry to repeat the current step. Choose Log Files to get more information about the error. Stop the option and continue later. Log files are written to /tmp/sapinst_instdir/SOLMAN72SR2/SOLMAN72SR2/ABAP/HDB/INSTALL/STD/ABAP.

entry.title: Re: Automate FI period open (OB52B) in SAP S/4HANA On premise
entry.content: Informative

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition赋能全景指南：2025年线上分享一览（大中华区中文版）
entry.content:
在当今快速变化的商业环境中，企业面临着如何高效利用云技术来提升运营效率和竞争力的挑战。GROW with SAP正是为了应对这一需求而推出的一套全面解决方案。它不仅整合了技术工具和服务，还提供了丰富的社区资源和学习材料，助力各种规模的企业成功采用云端ERP系统，实现智能化和可持续性的运营

GROW with SAP的核心是SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。这是一款功能全面的云端ERP解决方案，旨在优化企业流程，提升运营效率，并推动企业迈向智能化和可持续发展的未来。

作为Cloud ERP Product Success产品成功团队的一员，我们深知帮助客户和合作伙伴充分利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud解决方案的重要性。为此，我们精心设计了多种培训和支持资源，通过专业的产品专家深入讲解每一项功能，揭示其中的价值和创新带来的优势。

让我们一起探索我们提供的不同类型的线上分享产品专家分享，这些分享已经帮助许多企业成功迈向云端智能管理的新高度。

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 版本亮点前瞻系列博客

想要在市场变化前占得先机？这些早期发布系列分享为您提供了抢先了解最新功能和创新的机会。通过这些分享，您不仅能第一时间了解SAP S/4HANA Cloud的新特性，还能迅速应用于实际业务中，提升企业灵活应变的能力。

2. In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

如果您希望深入探讨SAP S/4HANA Cloud的应用，这个系列非常适合您。我们会详细讲解系统操作和优化方法，分享最佳实践案例，帮助您找到最适合自己企业的优化路径。这些分享让复杂的技术变得通俗易懂，使您能够轻松驾驭云端ERP系统。

3. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training

专为产品专家设计的培训分享，通过全面系统的学习，使您能够专业支持和指导客户，深入理解产品的核心价值。这不仅帮助您提升专业水平，还能够更好地为企业提供战略指导，增强企业的市场竞争力。

为了帮助您更好地了解每种分享的内容和优势，以下是一张分享概览表。如果您希望获取更多信息和定期更新，请访问相关的页面（链接如下）。

直播产品专家课程

覆盖内容

频率

目标受众

访问形式

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition版本亮点前瞻系列博客

讨论和演示即将发布的SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition主要创新

每半年一次

客户、合作伙伴、SAP内部员工

图文（SAP Community 博客）：

SAP ERP公有云 [SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] 2408 亮点前瞻

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 亮点前瞻

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2308 亮点前瞻

In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition GCR 产品深度赋能系列 – 大中华区

深入的知识传递课程，与发布周期无关

按需每月

客户、合作伙伴、SAP内部员工

线上直播和可供参考的录屏资料（需要有效的SAP Learning Hub许可证）：[新]日程2024：In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系列 - 合集

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training GCR – 大中华区产品专家培训

每半年一次

为期约7天的培训，覆盖广泛且深入的SAP S/4HANA Cloud版本知识

客户、合作伙伴和SAP内部员工

线上直播和可供参考的录屏资料（需要有效的SAP Learning Hub许可证）：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Product Expert Training GCR 产品专业培训中文系列合集

 

广泛覆盖：多领域、多行业的主题分享

我们不仅提供产品专家线上分享，还覆盖了众多业务线和行业相关主题，以及其他跨领域的课题。这些分享涵盖了以下多个领域：

- 人工智能（Artificial Intelligence）

- SAP用户体验（User Experience）

- Joule

- 财务管理

- 风险与合规管理（GRC）

- 制造业和离散行业

- 供应链管理

- 销售

- 企业组合与项目管理

- 专业服务

- 可持续发展

- 产品合规

- 环境健康与安全（EHS）

 

- 服务管理

- 采购与供应

- 资产管理

- 解决方案订单管理

- 消费品

- 零售业

- 主数据管理

- 技术话题

- SAP Activate

- SAP Cloud ALM

- 测试自动化工具

- Two-Tier 双层ERP

- SAP Central Business Configuration（CBC）

- 以及更多！

这些主题不仅触及到不同的业务领域，还包括最新的技术趋势和工具应用。无论您是想深入了解某一特定行业，还是希望掌握最新的技术发展，这些分享都能满足您的需求。

我们期待在未来的分享和支持中继续为您提供帮助，并通过不断创新的解决方案和实践，为企业的成功保驾护航。一起加入我们，紧跟技术潮流，打造智慧企业的未来！


2025年全年产品专家线上分享安排 - 预览版本（将随时调整）

随着企业对智能化和数字化需求的不断增多，我们也将继续在2025年为您提供丰富的产品专家线上分享。下图展示了我们在2025年全年的计划。

图1：2025年计划的产品专家线上分享概览。SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition的发布和更新周期遵循SAP的发布计划，如有变动，线上分享计划也可能会随之调整。

图1：2025年计划的产品专家线上分享【预览版本】概览。SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition的发布和更新周期遵循SAP的发布计划，如有变动，线上分享计划也可能会随之调整。

针对SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition的两次主要发布——2502和2508版本，我们计划在正式发布后的前1-2周，通过亮点前瞻系列博客为您展示和演示这些版本的亮点。

四月：我们计划举办今年的首次Product Expert Training 大中华区产品专家培训，重点是SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502版本。在2025年下半年（H2），我们还将计划针对SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2508版本的下一次产品专家培训。

月度深入探讨：根据需求，我们几乎每过几个月都会举办一些"In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" 产品深度赋能系列 – 大中华区，这个系列独立于发布周期，主要针对特定话题进行深入探讨，为您提供某些特定产品方面的全面洞察。该系列的话题选择主要通过利益相关者的建议，如果您有感兴趣的主题，欢迎在本博客文章中提出。

entry.title: OBXL setting- payment small differences are posted with Tax line/tax code
entry.content:
Dear team, we are facing a challenge clnt N62 SID 081 in posting the small customer payment differences to specified GL account maintained in tcode OBXL. The issue is when we post customer payment(F-28) the small difference which is under tolerance getting posted by tax code hence posting is generating separate tax line for small difference. 

The same testing I have performed in client N62 SID 041. But in this client the customer payment posting is not calculating tax and hence not generating separate tax line.

The requirement is that we do not want to calculate tax on small differences amount and if possible want to use default tax code for small differences in Clnt N62.081

I have also checked the GL setting under control data (FS00) for both clients. The setting is same and the used tax code is tax applicable

entry.title: Can't find freight order in warehouse
entry.content:
I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and wanted to make a transportation execution (scope item 6W3) for an IM warehouse. For this I created a sales order and based on it also a freight order. Then I sent the freight order to the carrier and set to "ready for transport execution". In the next step I wanted to load the freight order, but I get an error that the freight order can't be found in the warehouse.

Are there maybe some configurations to make this process work? This process works in the EWM warehouse.

Deleting the cookies, changing the browser or using the browser's incognito mode did not help.

Many thanks for your help,

Greetings, Emelie

entry.content: Hi  after editing the odjt, jdt1, oivl, and ivl1 tables using an SQL query, the system was unable to generate an accounts receivable (A/R) credit memo, show this error "Internal error (-5002) [Message 131-183]"

entry.title: Maintenance Item Have Different Start Cycle
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Our customer have requirement to create maintenance plan time based. We already test and explore it and we have a question.

Can we have a different start cycle for every maintenance item ? for example : in Maintenance Plan 1 we have 3 Maintenance Items with different Technical Object/Equipment. We want to have every maintenance item to have a different start cycle. Any suggestion to this case ?

Thank You!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management

Best Regards,

Michal Krawczyk

entry.title: Re: How to make product type group as mandatory field in...
entry.content:
Hi,

You may check the BAdI MM_PUR_S4_CTR_MODIFY_FLDCTRL Change of Field Control for Purchase Contract Header and Item which can be used to manage the field control properties at the header and item levels of the purchase contracts.

App Extensibility: Manage Purchase Contracts

Best regards,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: editing the journal entry table using SQL unable to g...
entry.content:
Hello,

The error you're encountering, "Internal error (-5002) [Message 131-183]," often indicates a data integrity issue in the system after the changes made to the odjt, jdt1, oivl, and ivl1 tables. This can happen if the modifications violate some expected database constraints or dependencies required for generating an accounts receivable (A/R) credit memo.

Here are a few steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve this issue:

Check the Integrity of Modified Data:

Ensure that the data you've modified in the odjt, jdt1, oivl, and ivl1 tables is valid and adheres to any constraints or relationships between these tables. For instance:
Ensure that entries in jdt1 are correctly linked to the odjt and oivl tables.
Verify that all fields required for generating the A/R credit memo (such as transaction types, amounts, and document numbers) are properly updated.
Rollback Changes if Possible:

If you have a backup or version control, consider rolling back the changes to the tables and testing the generation of the credit memo again to confirm that the modifications are the source of the issue.
Check for Missing or Invalid Records:

Ensure that any records in odjt, jdt1, oivl, and ivl1 are not missing critical data fields. Missing or incorrect foreign key values can prevent the A/R credit memo from being generated.
Review System Logs:

Check the system logs or trace logs (if available) to identify any additional error messages or warnings related to the credit memo generation process. This could provide more insight into the specific cause of the issue.
Validate System Configuration:

Verify if there are any system configurations or business logic rules that govern the creation of A/R credit memos. Some configurations or missing setups in the SAP or accounting system might interfere with the process.
Test the Query in Isolation:

Test the SQL query on a non-production environment with sample data to ensure that the modifications don't trigger the error, and examine the behavior in a controlled environment.

Best Regards
progressive agent login

entry.title: Navigating Central Procurement: Your Map to Integrated Success
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, streamlining procurement processes can mean the difference between success and stagnation. With the integration of SAP S/4HANA into your system landscape, you can achieve centralized procurement processes that span multiple ERP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP ERP. This powerful central procurement functionality enables your organization to operate more efficiently and effectively.

However, the wealth of features and processes within Central Procurement can sometimes feel overwhelming. From central contract distribution to Ariba integration scenarios, there are numerous aspects to consider and understand. As a product support expert, I’ve witnessed firsthand how the vast array of possibilities can leave customers feeling a bit lost.

To address this, I've created a comprehensive resource with @daniel_marlen designed to guide you through the intricacies of Central Procurement. After contributing to various wiki sites and knowledge-based articles (KBAs), I realized the need for a unified documentation that serves as a roadmap for understanding and utilizing Central Procurement effectively. The guide can be reached through the following KBA: 3544902 - Central Purchasing Troubleshooting - Guided Answers

Introducing: The Guided Answer Document for Central Procurement

The Guided Answer Document is a thoroughly crafted guide that consolidates all the essential information and processes related to Central Procurement. Here’s what you can expect:

With the Guided Answer Document, you no longer need to feel lost in the landscape of Central Procurement. This resource is designed to empower you, making your journey through centralized procurement processes smoother and more intuitive.

I encourage you to explore this comprehensive guide and make the most out of your SAP S/4HANA integration. Whether you are new to Central Procurement or a seasoned professional looking to optimize processes, this documentation will be an invaluable asset.

Stay tuned for updates and new insights as we continue to refine and expand this essential resource!

Best regards,
Tamás

entry.title: Information regarding the ECC6.0 EHP 0
entry.content:
I need the information regarding the SAP ECC 6.0 EHP 0
which type of SLES VERSION AND SPS VERSION is supported for SAP ECC 6.0 EHP 0

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content:
I prefer recipes that are simple with the fewest ingredients that are local staples. It's fun to try new things but it's no so fun to end up with a fridge or pantry full of half used sauces and other food stuff. 

This is a classic Sephardic dish called Keftes de Carne: meatballs in tomato sauce. It's an easy way to feed an army with minimal effort. 

  • Make the meatballs by combining the following into golf sized balls
    • 600 grams beef or other protein 
    • 2 eggs
    • 1/4 cup bread crumbs
    • 1/4 cup finely chopped parsley
    • half an onion, diced
    • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Coat the meatballs in flour, then dip them in eggs (beaten) before shallow frying on both sides.  (Tip: Add a carrot in the drying oil to stop the oil from burning!)
  • After frying, place the meatballs con a wire rack
  • When all the meatballs are done, place them in a very liquid simmering tomato sauce (can even be 2 jars of store bought sauce combine with 1/2 cup of water).
  • Cook on medium heat for 30 min or so, uncovered (I keep the lid half on). Spoon some sauce over the meatballs halfway through or turn over slowly. 
  • Add water to keep the sauce from turning too thick as needed. 
  • Serve with spanish rice (traditional) or even plain rice or a pasta if you prefer.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign the iRPA road to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi @Jana_dK 

We are performing some beta migration and the actual migration will be planned soon

Regards,

Mugesh P

entry.title: Re: Sustainable growth?
entry.content:
Indeed, 3% of growth would conduct to double global economy in 25 years (by 2050). Knowing that global economy is already putting too much pressure on the environment, we can tell easily that this is not possible to double it by 2050. Even if growth partially relies on service intangible value, services need physical flows as well in order to be delivered. 

An interesting book on this subject is Doughnut Economics: Seven Ways to Think Like a 21st-Century Economist by Kate Raworth.

entry.title: Re: New Account assignment is not show on Purchase Contract
entry.content:
Hi,

The custom account categories are not integrated on the fiori apps on current release. Manage Purchase Contracts app supports the following:

  • Sales Order (C)

  • Cost Center (K)

  • Project (P)

  • Third-Party (S)

  • Unknown (U)

  • Network (N)

  • Asset (A)

  • Order (F)

For more information, consult the provided documentation on the Feature Comparison for Manage Purchase Contracts App and Create Purchase Contracts(GUI).

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/625138d705ec482a9214b4ec534cf055.html?locale=en-US

Best regards,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC)
entry.content:
Hi Can you share some detail document if you have please .

I need to see the Bill of material object dependency details

entry.title: Re: Tcode : Stock in Transit
entry.content:
MB5T & MB5TD is a Stock in Transit report, means those Outbound Delivery against which the 101 GRN not yet posted, will show data in MB5T/MB5TD. Once you post the GRN, no data will show in In Transit Report. 

So pls check if you have posted GRN or not.

entry.title: Can I extend the standard field BEDNR of the ekpo table to 30 in length?
entry.content: Can I extend the standard field BEDNR of the ekpo table to 30  in length? Will it make any difference

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @buz 

unfortunately the screenshot of the network trace does not show the full paths which have been used. Therefore I can't get any helpful information out of it, except that the connection to the OData service does not work and returns 404.
What I can see from your MTA is that you are not using the new CDM based approach from this blog but the old approach with the old HTML5 provider from my former blog series (actually this question better fits there).
What can't be seen from the MTA is, how you forward the OData requests to your backend. A route for this would be part of the xs-app.json. How exactly does this route look like?
I assume you point to the destination RoyCustDeclaration-srv-api which points to your backend. Please verify that the correct URL is set there.
Please also check the approuter logs for the failing requests.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: How to configure spot reward budget based on department
entry.content:
Hi team,

Is there a spectific way to configure spot reward budget based on department. 

 

For Example : For Dept 1 Bused is 5000 USD

Dept 2 budget is 2000 USD

Or do we have any alternative solution for this requirement.

Is it possible to restrict number of nomination for each department
